source_issue.title: IWorkingCopy interface is preserved in copy of an item

summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue addresses a bug where the `IWorkingCopy` interface is still maintained in a copied version of an item in Plone. This can create issues if you rely on this interface for custom views or other functionalities.

## What needs to be done
The goal is to ensure that when a working copy is copied, it does not retain the `IWorkingCopy` interface. This involves implementing a fix—potentially within the `plone.app.iterate` package.

## Helpful background
When you create and copy a working copy item (a user-editable version of a content item), it currently retains the `IWorkingCopy` interface. This can lead to confusion and malfunction in systems dependent on identifying true working copies. There are suggestions on how to handle this via event handlers to properly erase the interface from the copied item.

## Good first steps
1. Review the existing code and comments, particularly the provided event handler code.
2. Test if the issue still persists in your local environment.
3. Implement the suggested event handler and test it to ensure it removes the interface as expected.
4. Consider documenting your findings and reaching out for review if you need guidance or further collaboration.
